title: Entry-level employability is dense; in-work progression is thin
body: The map shows multiple organisations delivering at J4 (entry to work) in central Cardiff, but only a small number active at J5 (in-work progression). The handoff at 6-8 weeks is largely unsupported.
potentialWorkstream: A coordinated 6-month in-work support offer that picks up where the J4 programmes hand off — see the illustrative Splott J5 progression workstream above.

title: Multiple organisations, no shared referral pathway
body: Three or more organisations serve the same J4 cohort in central Cardiff with no formal referral protocol between them. Young people retell their story to each. Lived-experience insight confirms the cost of this.
potentialWorkstream: A shared lightweight referral protocol owned by the J4 cluster — small enough to pilot, structured enough to remove the repeated-retelling cost.

title: City-centre concentration, suburban thinness
body: Provision clusters in CF10/CF11. Communities in CF3, CF5 and the outer wards depend on transport to reach support, and bus times do not align with shift work. The Splott insight is one example of the pattern.
potentialWorkstream: A transport-aligned outreach offer co-designed with Cardiff Bus and the outer-ward partnerships — picks up the Splott late-shift gap as a concrete first case.

title: Corporate social-value offers do not yet align to surfaced needs
body: Several corporate partners (Admiral, PWC and others in the wider coalition) sit in J4 as Involved. Their offers default to common formats (mentoring, work experience) rather than the specific needs the map surfaces - ESOL capacity, six-month-in-work support, ALN transition.
potentialWorkstream: A short employer-coalition exercise that maps each major Cardiff employer's current offer against the three specific needs surfaced here - and reshapes one offer per employer.
